title: GC.SuppressFinalize should not be called
description: "GC.SuppressFinalize" should not be calledAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?GC.SuppressFinalizerequests that the system not call the finalizer for the specified object. This should only be done when implementingDisposeas part of theDispose Pattern.This rule raises an issue whenGC.SuppressFinalizeis called outside that pattern.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects GC.SuppressFinalize calls outside of Dispose method pattern
logic.triggering_conditions: Triggers when: GC.SuppressFinalize is called in methods NOT named Dispose
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes: calls within Dispose methods, Dispose(bool) pattern, comments, test code
logic.reasoning: GC.SuppressFinalize should only be called as part of the Dispose pattern in IDisposable.Dispose methods. Calling it elsewhere is incorrect and confusing. This rule detects GC.SuppressFinalize calls but excludes those within methods named 'Dispose' or matching the Dispose pattern. Pattern matches 'GC.SuppressFinalize(' while excluding lines containing 'void Dispose' or 'Dispose(bool'.

should_not_trigger: protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing) { GC.SuppressFinalize(this); }
